TextENGNew: ...It was a night of fitful sleep.
...I spent what felt like an endless night doing nothing but tossing and turning.
Even so, I must have drifted off at some point... and didn't notice how high the sun had climbed.
...I wrestled down the temptation to go back to sleep.
It was then I finally noticed the phone had been ringing.
The time was nearly half past ten in the morning.
Far too late for a wake-up call.
``Akasaka-san?@k@vS05/11/100700341. Good morning, it's Ooishi.@k@vS05/11/100700342. Nfu fu fu!``
Ooishi's voice first thing in the morning was a rather potent jolt.
It rang painfully through my still-sensitive ears.
...But thanks to that shock, I was wide awake in an instant.
``...Ah... oh, hello.@k@vS05/13/101300412. Good morning.``
``Hmm~?@k@vS05/11/100700344. Don't tell me you just woke up?@k@vS05/11/100700345. Business trip or not, you should really be up during working hours.``
``You're absolutely right.@k@vS05/13/101300414. ...I'll be more careful from now on.``
``I might have something else that'll wake you up.@k@|@y@vS05/11/100700348. ...An interesting item turned up in the lost and found from the Hinamizawa police box.``
``Lost and found?``
``I'll save the details for when you get here.@k@vS05/11/100700350. Can you make it before noon?``
``N-no, I'll come right away!``
Ooishi already understood my mission.
...And if he was calling something a 'wake-up' find...@kI couldn't imagine it would be irrelevant.
Frankly, based on Sato-san's information from yesterday,@kknowing my cover was potentially blown, I could no longer waltz in and out of Hinamizawa freely.
...In other words, my investigation had hit a wall.
A potential lead arriving at a time like this was nothing short of miraculous.
changeReason: Original says 'stroke of good luck'; JPN 幸運としか言いようがない is closer to 'nothing short of a stroke of luck,' but new changes to 'miraculous,' slightly overstating.

TextENGNew: I gave my face a quick wash, grabbed my jacket, hailed a taxi, and headed for the Okinomiya station.
``Oh, there you are.@k@vS05/11/100700352. The genius mahjong player has arrived.``
``Sorry I'm late.@k@vS05/13/101300418. ...So what exactly turned up?``
With no desire whatsoever to reminisce about last night's mahjong, I pressed him to get to the point.
Ooishi, apparently expecting as much, wasted no time getting to business.
``...As I mentioned, something arrived this morning from the Hinamizawa police box's lost and found.``
Items turned in at the police box were sent to the Okinomiya station after the paperwork was completed.
Ooishi pulled 'it' from a drawer -- sealed in a plastic bag with a label attached -- and set it on the desk.
``...A wallet?``
``Appears so.@k@vS05/11/100700355. ...There was some change inside, but no bills.@k@vS05/11/100700356. A pickpocket probably took what they wanted and tossed the rest.``
I couldn't immediately see how this wallet, found so far from Tokyo, could be connected to the minister's grandson's kidnapping.
...If it had drawn Ooishi's interest to this degree,@kit would need to be something dramatic -- like the wallet actually belonging to the missing grandson.
...That was absurd.
...Things didn't work out that conveniently.
Most likely, the wallet just had initials written on it.
Like 'T.I.' -- the initials of the minister's grandson, Toshiki Inukai.
Thinking along those lines, I didn't consider this wallet to be as noteworthy as Ooishi's excitement suggested.
Ooishi put on a pair of disposable vinyl gloves, then offered me a pair as well.
I didn't see the wallet as that important, so I questioned whether gloves were even necessary -- but not wanting to waste Ooishi's goodwill, I put them on.
Ooishi opened the bag, pulled out the wallet, and turned it over to show me the back.
...There, just as I'd imagined, initials were engraved.
But they were slightly more detailed than what I'd expected --@k@|@y 'Toshiki I.' was inscribed on it.
'See?' Ooishi grinned at me.
...That alone isn't enough to identify it as the minister's grandson's, I told him calmly.
...This wallet was surely nothing. Just Ooishi jumping the gun.
There was absolutely no way the missing grandson's wallet would turn up in this remote location at such a convenient time.
...That's right -- the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance had nothing to do with this case to begin with.
changeReason: New translates 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟 as 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' rather than 'Onigafuchi Guardians,' changing the organization name rendering.

TextENGNew: ...So there was no way this wallet belonged to the grandson...
Why I wanted so badly to dismiss it, even I couldn't quite explain.
Even though every detail of this supposedly top-secret case -- from its occurrence to my dispatch -- had apparently been leaked.
changeReason: Original says leak happened 'before I had even been dispatched'; new correctly captures JPN 発生から自分の派遣に至るまで meaning 'from its occurrence to my dispatch' — i.e., every stage was leaked.

TextENGNew: ...In a situation like this, how could I claim with certainty that it was unrelated?
Shouldn't I be sharpening my focus and taking this wallet seriously?
...It had been only a few days since I arrived in Hinamizawa.
...But the cascade of intense events during those few days had worn my nerves just a little... just slightly thin, perhaps.@kI gave my head a small shake, dispelling the stray thoughts.
Ooishi unzipped the wallet and opened it up.
Inside was a jumble of coins and crumpled receipts.
From that mess, Ooishi extracted a bent paper card.
...The moment I saw it, my breath caught.
changeReason: Original says 'my heart stopped'; JPN 呼吸が一度止まる means 'my breathing stopped/caught' — new correctly translates this physiological reaction.

TextENGNew: It was a dental examination card.
The name 'Toshiki Inukai' and the age listed.@k...A perfect match.
The dental office's location... was in Tokyo.
...There was no need for further explanation.
...How far was it from Tokyo to Hinamizawa?
Even taking the bullet train and transferring to a car, it took hours upon hours.
So why would a wallet containing a dental card from a Tokyo clinic turn up in Hinamizawa?
Could it belong to a tourist visiting from Tokyo?
Or maybe a villager happened to visit a dentist while sightseeing in Tokyo?@kNo, maybe it got mixed in with someone's luggage by accident...
My mind scrambled desperately to find a reason why this wallet might not belong to Toshiki Inukai.
It was as though the wallet simply must not be his...
But as those margins of error were eliminated one by one...@k...the haze that had surrounded the wallet thinned, and its significance came into sharper and sharper focus.
I felt electricity crackling up my spine.
My heart rate quickened, and a fine sweat began to seep from every pore...
...To Ooishi, I probably looked slack-jawed and dumbstruck.
``...Did you contact this dental office?``
``That's your job, Akasaka-san.@k@vS05/11/100700360. I haven't gone that far.``
``...When do you estimate this wallet was dropped?``
changeReason: Original asks 'when did wallet show up'; JPN いつ頃のもの asks about the age/vintage of the wallet (when it was lost/dropped), which the new correctly captures.

TextENGNew: ``A villager picked it up.@k@vS05/11/100700362. Yesterday, apparently.``
``No, I mean when do you think the wallet was actually dropped?``
``It rained here exactly one week ago.@k@vS05/11/100700364. The contents show no water damage, so it was dropped six days ago at most.``
``Who at this station knows about this wallet?``
``The officer at the Hinamizawa police box who first handled it.@k@vS05/11/100700366. Two or three people in lost-and-found processing.@k@vS05/11/100700367. And then me and you.``
``...May I borrow a phone?``
``Be my guest.@k@vS05/11/100700371. Dial zero for an outside line.``
I dialed the phone number printed on the examination card.
Inside my head, my pulse pounded like a drum.
...Could something this absurd really be happening?!
The addresses on the receipts and cards in the wallet were all consistent with the grandson's living area.
The name on the dental card was a match,@kand the estimated timeframe for when the wallet was dropped aligned with the period since the kidnapping.
Of course, one could laugh all of that off as a string of coincidences.
...None of it constituted strong evidence on its own.
...But the phone call I was about to make would carry far more weight than all that circumstantial evidence combined...
``...Hello, sorry for the wait.@k@vS05/00/nur00002. This is Nakagawa Dental.``

``Hello. This is the Okinomiya Police Station.@k@vS05/13/101300427. We have a lost wallet here and we're trying to locate the owner.@k@vS05/13/101300428. I'd like to verify some information from one of your patient cards.``
``...Yes.@k@vS05/13/101300431. The name is Toshiki Inukai.@k@vS05/13/101300432. If I give you the card number, could you look up the phone number on file?``
Still holding the receiver, I used my free hand to pull a notebook from my breast pocket.
...I opened it to the page where I'd recorded the grandson's home address and phone number.
``Thank you.@k@vS05/13/101300436. ...Yes, go ahead.``
I cross-referenced the phone number being read aloud from the other end with the one recorded in my notebook.
...Partway through, an eerie feeling crept up behind me, and I spun around.
My eyes met Ooishi's.
...Ooishi looked puzzled, wondering what had gotten into me.
Why had I felt that unsettling presence behind me?
...Because the person on the other end of the line...@kseemed to be reading the numbers as though peering directly into my notebook...
My report seemed to have sent headquarters into considerable shock.
No matter how many times they questioned the authenticity of this unmistakable evidence of the grandson's presence, I could only tell them it was real...
The lead investigator took the call first@kand bombarded me with probing, nitpicking questions about the evidence -- which, I had to admit, seemed almost too perfect.
...But as his doubts were swiftly and conclusively refuted one after another, he fell silent.@k...At last, the receiver was passed to the section chief.
By the time the section chief picked up, the background murmur that had been audible on the other end had died away, replaced by dead silence.
After asking the same questions about the wallet that the lead investigator had, the section chief spoke quietly.
``Understood.@k@vS05/00/104200040. We'll dispatch several people as reinforcements.@k@vS05/00/104200041. They should arrive by this evening.``

``Akasaka-kun, please continue your on-site investigation.@k@vS05/00/104200043. Gather information about the area where the wallet was found.@k@vS05/00/104200044. Our people will join you when they arrive.``
The fact that they were sending the lead investigator, Kanou, meant the section chief had deemed the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance's involvement plausible.
Even so, he would likely remain half-skeptical until Kanou -- whom he trusted -- had examined the evidence with his own eyes.
``How'd it go?``
``Headquarters is dispatching reinforcements.@k@vS05/13/101300438. I've been ordered to continue the on-site investigation.@k@vS05/13/101300439. ...Where exactly was the wallet found?``

``The Takatsudo area, apparently.@k@vS05/11/100700374. ...Here's Okinomiya.@k@vS05/11/100700375. Keep going and here's Hinamizawa.@k@vS05/11/100700376. And Takatsudo is way past that, deep in the mountains.``
Ooishi pointed at the wall map as he explained where the wallet had been found.
Takatsudo, where the wallet was discovered, was even more remote than Hinamizawa.
``There's hardly anyone living around Takatsudo.@k@vS05/11/100700378. It's an old abandoned village.@k@vS05/11/100700379. Just scattered empty houses.``
``...Ooishi-san.@k@vS05/13/101300441. Could I ask you to guide me to Takatsudo?``
``Sure, no problem.@k@vS05/11/100700381. You can't exactly come and go from Hinamizawa freely on your own, after all.@k@vS05/11/100700382. You need someone who knows the area.``
Having Ooishi agree to come along was deeply reassuring.
...If Sato-san's information was to be believed, my cover was compromised.
Whether the villagers had connected 'the rookie from Public Security' to the tourist who'd visited their village yesterday -- I couldn't be sure.
But assuming they hadn't would be dangerously optimistic.
Even by the most cursory reasoning, Takatsudo -- where the wallet was found -- couldn't be ruled out as a potential hideout for the kidnappers.
The Takatsudo district had no inhabited houses and wasn't along any main road, so even locals had no reason to venture there.
...It was a forgotten ghost town at the end of a dead-end road -- the perfect description.
If you wanted to hold a kidnapped child and lie low, you couldn't ask for a more convenient location.
And Takatsudo could only be reached by passing through Hinamizawa.
These converging factors elevated the district's importance with each passing thought.
I could no longer force myself to deny it.
...There was no longer any doubt that the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance -- or someone acting on their behalf -- was behind this.
If that were the case...@kthe fact that this wallet had come into police hands might have already leaked.
...These were people who even knew the movements of the Public Security Bureau.@kSomething like this wallet would be well within their awareness.
And if so... the enemy might already be lying in wait.
I didn't have the courage -- or rather, the recklessness -- to charge alone into enemy territory within enemy territory.
``Without your company, Ooishi-san, I wouldn't be able to set foot in that village so easily.@k@vS05/13/101300443. Having your cooperation is a tremendous help.@k@vS05/13/101300444. Thank you.``
``Na ha ha ha.@k@vS05/11/100700384. To be honest, Hinamizawa isn't exactly a place I'd choose to visit for fun.@k@vS05/11/100700385. The locals don't like police very much.``
``Still, I can't turn down a request from a friend I've shared a mahjong table with.@k@vS05/11/100700387. Nfu fu fu...!``
Ooishi grinned and gave my back a hearty slap.
It struck me less as a sense of duty and more as someone who'd gotten caught up in the ride.
But none of that mattered.@kRight now, his willingness was something I was truly grateful for.
``Then shall we head out right away?``
...If the enemy knew the wallet had been recovered by the police...
They would move the hostage to another location.@kThere wasn't a moment to lose.
But Ooishi, despite our being about to leave, began taking off his jacket.
``...What are you doing?``
``Just a precaution.@k@vS05/11/100700389. If we're going to reach into the fire, we ought to dress for it.@k@vS05/11/100700390. You should put one on too.``
What Ooishi pulled from beneath his desk was a stab-proof vest...
His name was Toshiki Inukai.
Grandson of the Minister of Construction, Inukai.
The only reason he was here was that single fact -- nothing more, nothing less.
Aside from gym and art class, which he found somewhat challenging, his grades were above average in everything.
That was thanks to a tutor who visited twice a week.
Aside from still being unable to do a pull-up on the horizontal bar, there wasn't much about school he disliked.
He was fully aware that his grandfather was a cabinet minister.

But he also knew that most people didn't find the connection between them particularly interesting.@kHe disliked it when people brought up his grandfather and called him 'the Minister of Construction.'
And he believed he knew at least a little more about what the Minister of Construction actually did than the average adult.
What that meant was that the harder you worked to improve people's lives... the more enemies you sometimes made.
He'd learned that firsthand during his stint as class representative.
His grandfather had many enemies.
...And since that trouble might someday spill over onto him, he had to live with extra caution.@kThat's what his parents had taught him.
That was why... when he was kidnapped, he immediately understood that this was precisely one of those situations.
He understood that they weren't treating him as Toshiki Inukai the individual, but as the grandson of the Minister of Construction.
It wasn't because of any fault of his own -- it was an unavoidable disaster born of his grandfather's work.
...The distinction might seem trivial, but recognizing that he bore no personal blame gave his heart the smallest measure of calm.
At first, naturally, he was gripped by age-appropriate terror, shrinking in fear at the thought of what might happen to him.
But confined to lying on the ground in restraints with nothing else to do, his mind inevitably began to wander.
First, he considered why he had been brought here.
That answer came easily.
He was a hostage, leverage to coerce his grandfather, the minister.@kThat much was beyond doubt.
He might not fully understand what the Minister of Construction did, but it was easy to imagine that threatening him could yield enormous gains.
He respected his grandfather more than most boys his age.
He genuinely believed his grandfather was one of the dozen or so most important people in all of Japan.
That was precisely why he couldn't forgive those who would threaten his beloved grandfather -- and above all, he couldn't forgive that his own body was being used as the instrument of that threat.
His grandfather was truly busy, rarely able to coordinate schedules even for family gatherings.
That was why, whenever he found even a sliver of free time, he would drop by unannounced.
And, apologizing for missing New Year's or summer vacation, he would lavish affection on his grandson.
...There were certainly times when people looked at him unfavorably because his grandfather was a minister and politician.@kBut he had never once wished his grandfather would quit politics.
Precisely because he respected him, he wished from the bottom of his heart that his grandfather would keep doing his important work.
But now... he had been kidnapped, and his grandfather was being threatened.
His grandfather, who said Toshiki was the apple of his eye... must be suffering terribly.
He might do anything to save his grandson.
...He might even compromise his own political principles if it meant rescuing him.
His grandfather might capitulate to the kidnappers' demands to save his grandson...
When Toshiki thought about that...@k...an indescribable fury welled up inside him.
He couldn't forgive that his captivity was causing his beloved grandfather pain, and above all, he couldn't forgive these cowards who would use such despicable means to threaten him.
As those feelings filled his heart, the fear gradually began to recede...
The first thing that came to mind was the fantasy any boy would dream up.
...Breaking free of his bonds, pummeling his despicable captors, and marching them to the police.
The heroic images of protagonists from all the manga he'd read flashed through his mind.
In his imagination, he slipped his bonds with ease, danced around his captors like a butterfly, and delivered devastating blows like a bee's sting, one after another.
It was nothing but fantasy, of course, but with his vision blocked and nothing to look at, it was more absorbing than any anime could have been.
In fact, by occupying so much of his confinement with these daydreams, he wasn't suffering nearly as much as his captors probably imagined.
But eventually he grew tired of the fantasies, and finally realized that filling his head with unrealistic scenarios wasn't solving anything.
...Yes -- he wanted to do something for his grandfather, who was being threatened because of him.
That his being held here was causing his respected grandfather pain was the most unforgivable thing of all, and the most frustrating.
...And then, he realized something.
He didn't need to beat up his captors -- there was a far simpler way to save his grandfather.
He could remove himself as a hostage from their hands.
If he disappeared from here, his captors would have nothing left to threaten his grandfather with.
They had restrained him, but they hadn't been violent, and they never missed a meal.
...In other words, he was nothing more than a bargaining chip for their threats.@kHurting him -- let alone killing him -- was not their goal.
As long as he behaved, no harm would come to him.
Recognizing that gave him tremendous courage.
And once his thinking had reached this point, the rest followed naturally.
Toshiki Inukai had now resolved to escape.
His ears had been stuffed with sponge-like material, serving as makeshift earplugs.
...In reality, though, they did little to block sound, and his captors' conversations reached him clearly.
Recognizing this as a small advantage, he pretended he couldn't hear anything whenever they spoke to him.
His captors, believing his hearing was blocked, spoke freely about all manner of things without bothering to lower their voices.@kThanks to that, he was able to gather a great deal of information.
First of all... this wasn't Tokyo.
It seemed to be quite far away, deep in the countryside and nestled in the mountains.
Judging by how inconvenienced his captors seemed by their living conditions, he could deduce that this place was far from any town.
And whenever they went out to buy food or supplies, they always took the car.
...This fact gave him pause.@kThe elementary self-defense technique taught at school -- screaming for help and running blindly toward anyone nearby -- wouldn't work here.
His mental escape scenario hit a wall.
Someone who could protect him -- that was his finish line.
The farther that finish line was, the lower his chances of a successful escape.
A desolate, unfamiliar place deep in the mountains.
A location where you couldn't even go shopping without a car.
...In these conditions... escape would be extremely difficult.
...He grew disheartened, and for a while retreated back into his original fantasy.@kThe one where he was a manga hero beating up the bad guys.
But... even as those fantasies occupied his mind...@khis heart refused to surrender, and somewhere in the back of his thoughts, he kept searching for a way out.@k...And finally, he found one.
If the finish line was too far to reach... he just had to bring the finish line closer.
The finish line meant someone who could protect him.@kIn other words, anyone who wasn't one of his captors.
If he faked a serious illness, his captors might take him to a hospital -- or bring a doctor here.
They would probably resist the idea.@kBut, as he already understood, the hostage was their most precious asset.
If the unthinkable happened and he died, they would lose all leverage.
He was fortunate to have an experience that would help him convincingly fake a serious condition.
Late last year.@kHe'd undergone surgery after a traffic accident.
The surgical scar was still clearly visible on his lower abdomen.
He recalled the pain and anguish of that time.
Having actually lived through it would lend his performance an overwhelming realism that acting alone never could.@k...He had great confidence in his ability to fake this illness.
``...Nggghhhhhh...@k@vS05/00/toshi00005. ...Gggghhhhhh......``
His captors initially assumed it was a signal that he needed the bathroom.
They untied only his arms and tried to lead him to the washroom, still blindfolded.
But he wouldn't stand up.
His freed hands only clutched his abdomen as he writhed in agony.
``...Hey, what's wrong?@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1012. Is it your stomach?``
With his earplugs in, he was supposed to be unable to hear his captors' questions.
He nearly nodded reflexively and had to stop himself just in time.
``Whass the matter?``
``...The kid's clutching his stomach.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1014. Must be a stomachache.``
His captors stared down at him as he suffered for a while.
One seemed to be deliberating what to do, while the other appeared to suspect he might be faking.
``Whass wrong, boy?@k@vS05/14/104700020. Yer tummy hurtin'?``
``His ears are plugged -- he can't hear you.``
``Well, that's a problem.@k@vS05/14/104700022. ...We got any medicine for stomachaches?@k@vS05/14/104700023. Should I go to the pharmacy and pick somethin' up?``
``There's all kinds of stomach medicine, though.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1017. We can't just buy something random without knowing his symptoms...``

``He's holdin' his gut -- prob'ly constipated.@k@vS05/14/104700025. Buy 'im some laxatives and it'll sort itself out.@k@vS05/14/104700026. Even if he takes 'em by mistake, worst that happens is a bad case of the runs.``
``Don't just assume.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1019. What if it's something like appendicitis?@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1020. That'd be bad.``
One of the captors crouched beside the boy, who was doubled over in pain.
``...Poor kid.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1023. ...Whoa, he's drenched in sweat...@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1024. Something's really wrong.``
Noticing that his captors had taken an interest in his abdomen, he pretended to accidentally lift his shirt... exposing the surgical scar on his belly.
With his eyes still covered by packing tape, he couldn't see how they reacted to the scar.
But from their stunned silence that followed, he could tell he'd gotten exactly the reaction he'd hoped for.
``...Hey, this is bad.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1028. This scar -- it's not old.``
``...Does the kid have a fever?@k@vS05/14/104700028. Got a thermometer?``
``Of course we don't.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1030. But look at all this sweat...@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1031. Here, feel his forehead -- he's burning up.``
``...I've heard that prolonged stress can aggravate old wounds.``
``Then what do we do?!@k@vS05/14/104700030. Are ya sayin' medicine won't fix this?!``

``...I don't know what surgery this scar's from.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1034. ...But either way... this is serious.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1035. If it's a complication from the surgery, regular medicine won't do a thing.``
``...This is bad.@k@vS05/14/104700032. ...This is really, really bad...``
``...We should get a doctor to look at him.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1037. At this rate, there's a real chance he could just drop dead on us...``
Toshiki Inukai, hearing those words, knew things were progressing exactly as he'd planned. Smiling inwardly, he redoubled his act.
He didn't need to see to know his captors were panicking, running around like headless chickens cursing the complication.
``...Should we take him to a clinic?``
``No... that's no good.@k@vS05/14/104700034. ...Better to bring the doctor here.``
``Dr. Irie, you mean?@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1040. ...Is that safe?@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1041. Will he keep quiet?``
``...It'd be worse if somethin' happened to the hostage.@k@vS05/14/104700036. Can't be helped -- this is an emergency!``
One of the captors rushed out and drove off.
The remaining man, visibly flustered, was mopping the sweat from Toshiki's brow with a handkerchief.
...So they were bringing a doctor.
As long as the doctor wasn't an accomplice, that doctor was his finish line.@kThe person he needed to appeal to for help.
His captors would surely be on guard against exactly that.
How he would act in the moment --
...it would all come down to instinct.
...Some of the sweat beading on his forehead was from that very tension...
Then, the packing tape covering his eyes and ears was ripped away.
The blinding light he hadn't seen in days made his eyes ache painfully.
After that, the suffocating gag was also removed.
``...Hang in there, kid.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1043. We're bringing a doctor right now.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1044. Just hold on.``
``...Uhhhhnnnnn...@k@vS05/00/toshi00008. ...Gggghhhhhh...``
The doctor was coming... the doctor was coming...
How would he ask for help? How would he escape his captors' hands?@kA maelstrom of thoughts swirled through his mind.
``...I think you already know this...@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1046. ...but don't say anything unnecessary to the doctor.@k@vS05/49/VT2_13hibari1047. ...Got it?``
Toshiki Inukai nodded through his feigned pain, showing a display of obedience...
His intentions and the enemy's intentions.
...Whether they doubted his illness or not, they understood what his goal would be -- and would try to prevent it.
His stomach actually contracted from the stress of this gamble, and for a moment it felt like his pain was becoming real.@kOr perhaps it was.
But there was no reason to panic...
Even if he failed in his approach to the doctor, he would lose nothing.
Well, almost nothing -- he would lose this particular tactic of faking illness to establish contact with a doctor.
If he could think of other methods later, the loss would be insignificant.
But at this moment, he couldn't think of any plan easier or more dramatic than this one.
...The more he thought about it... the clearer it became that this was his first and last gamble!
Eventually... he heard the sound of a car approaching once more.
The hurried sounds of car doors opening and closing.@kMultiple sets of footsteps rushing toward him.
A young man in a white medical coat.
Upon seeing the boy, a look of suspicion crossed his face.
``...Who is this?``
``...He's not a child from the village, is he?``
The captors didn't answer.@k...From their silence, the young doctor seemed to realize this was no ordinary situation.
One of the captors spoke in a threatening tone.
``This don't concern you, doc.``
For a while, the doctor and the captors glared at each other in silence.
...But unable to bear the sight of the boy continuing to suffer, the doctor -- just as the captors hoped -- dropped his line of questioning.
``...I'm concerned about this old surgical scar.@k@vS05/10/101000005. What kind of operation was it?``
``...We don't know.``
``Ideally, I should bring him back to the clinic.``
``Doc, we really can't do that...``
The doctor insisted on taking the boy back to his clinic, but the captors simply shook their heads in silent refusal.
...It was a faint hope, and the captors' refusal was entirely predictable.
At that moment, his eyes met the doctor's.
The doctor, sensing something abnormal about the boy's pain, appeared to suspect a serious condition.
``...Are you all right?@k@vS05/10/101000008. I'm a doctor.@k@vS05/10/101000009. When was this surgery performed?``
``...Nngh... guh...@k@vS05/00/toshi00010. ...Last year... in the winter... from an accident...``
He couldn't let the doctor see through his act here and now.
He had to find some way to maintain the connection, convey that he was being held captive, and plead for rescue.
``I'm going to palpate the affected area, all right?@k@vS05/10/101000014. If it hurts anywhere, tell me.``
``...OWWWWWWWW!!!``
Seeing how extremely painful the area seemed to be... the doctor crossed his arms and sank into thought...
Somehow, he had to get himself to a hospital.
For Toshiki Inukai, this was the performance of a lifetime...
